text: 2nd Law: heavier objects need more force to accelerate — why trucks accelerate slower than motorcycles

Newton's three laws (1687, Principia) form the foundation of classical mechanics. 1st Law (inertia): object at rest stays at rest; in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by force. 2nd Law: F = ma (force equals mass times acceleration). 3rd Law: every action has equal and opposite reaction.
1st Law: seatbelt prevents you from continuing forward when car stops — your body wants to keep moving (inertia).
2nd Law: heavier objects need more force to accelerate — why trucks accelerate slower than motorcycles.
3rd Law: rocket pushes exhaust down, exhaust pushes rocket up.
